mappings.rationale: The primary VSI for this section, directly covering heredity, genome, variation, and the combined action of heredity and environment (A.3).
Covers genes, cell division, DNA sequencing, gene expression, sex linkage (B.1), the genetic code and mutations (B.2), and natural selection (C.2).
Covers the chemical and molecular nature of genes (B.2) including DNA sequencing, the genetic code, messenger RNA, and applications of molecular genetics.
Addresses molecular genetics (B.2) including DNA structure, gene expression and regulation, with historical context from Crick, Franklin, Mendel, and Watson.
Covers population genetics (C.1), genetic drift and gene frequency changes, natural selection as evolutionary agent (C.2), and artificial selection (C.3).
Covers chromosomes and genes as the cellular basis of heredity (B.1), including the nucleus, cell division, and how genetic material is transmitted.
Covers the historical foundation of heredity and evolution including Darwin's natural selection theory (C.2), species concept, and Wallace's contributions.
Directly relevant to artificial selection for genetic improvements of humankind (C.3), covering Francis Galton, heredity, and population-level selection programs.
Covers artificial selection of domesticated animals and cultivated plants (C.3), including genetically modified organisms as applications of molecular genetics.
Addresses modern applications of molecular genetics (B.2) including genetic engineering and genetically modified organisms relevant to artificial selection (C.3).
Examines conceptual foundations of heredity including the species concept, essentialism vs. population thinking, and nature-nurture interactions (A.3).
Covers genetics of microorganisms including bacterial gene transfer, DNA sequencing, and polymerase chain reaction as tools of molecular genetics (B.2).
